events.title: World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk
events.description: Four decades after AIDS first emerged as a global crisis, world leaders, advocates and community representatives gathered at UN Headquarters on Monday issued a stark warning: progress against HIV is slowing just as financial pressures and shrinking support threaten to reverse decades of gains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk

events.title: World News in Brief: UN launches Hormuz evacuation plan, UNICEF youth champion killed in Gaza, Lebanon ceasefire ‘largely holding’
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) will begin implementing an evacuation plan for more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, the UN agency announced on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: UN launches Hormuz evacuation plan, UNICEF youth champion killed in Gaza, Lebanon ceasefire ‘largely holding’

events.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three, injures 20
events.description: Investigators said one of the teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at a high school had regularly used the GoreBox gaming app Philippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian nation killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators said that of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city, one had regularly played the game, which allows players to use various weapons and depicts graphic violence. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three, injures 20

events.title: Global learning losses threaten an entire generation
events.description: Conflict, displacement and climate shocks are disrupting education for an estimated 258 million school-aged children and adolescents worldwide, raising fears that millions risk losing not only years of schooling but future opportunities altogether, according to a new report released Tuesday.

events.title: Iran war live: Trump, Tehran disagree over nuclear inspections, Hormuz
events.description: US and Iran offer conflicting statements as negotiators push towards reaching a final, comprehensive deal.

events.title: Fears grow for civilians as paramilitary group closes in on strategic Sudanese city

events.title: North Korea's Kim claims progress on nuclear-armed navy as new warship is placed into service

events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea's Kim claims progress on nuclear-armed navy as new warship is placed into service

events.title: Niger becomes the third country to leave the International Criminal Court
events.description: Niger has formally left the International Criminal Court after accusing the global tribunal of what it says is selective justice

events.title: Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report

events.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
events.description: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspiracy theories. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal

events.title: Report details widespread use of sexual violence in Sudan war
events.description: A new UN human rights report issued on Tuesday details the brutality and scale of conflict-related sexual violence in Sudan since war erupted in April 2023 and its profound, long-term impacts on victims, families and communities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Report details widespread use of sexual violence in Sudan war

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
events.description: Deadly drone and missile attacks in Ukraine over the weekend represented a “dangerous cycle of escalation” in Russia’s full-scale four-year-old war, the Security Council heard on Monday from Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General in the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs and Peace Operations, while Edem Wosornu, crisis response director at the UN relief office, OCHA, warned that “the choices made here can mean the difference between lives saved or lives lost.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine

events.title: Gay students extorted with threat of being ‘outed’ in home countries, Victorian hate crime inquiry hears
events.description: LGBTQ+ support service says men from countries where homosexuality is illegal are targeted for attacks via dating apps Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast International students from countries where homosexuality is illegal are being lured into meetings via gay dating apps before being assaulted and extorted with threats of being outed, a Victorian inquiry into hate crimes has heard. Since June 2024, police identified 95 attacks targeting gay and bisexual men across Victoria, resulting in 42 arrests, Thorne Harbour Health’s chief executive, Chad Hughes, told a hate crimes inquiry on Wednesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gay students extorted with threat of being ‘outed’ in home countries, Victorian hate crime inquiry hears

events.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
events.description: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

events.title: Trains resume after radio issue resolved, Deutsche Bahn says
events.description: Train services across Germany have resumed following a technical meltdown on Tuesday night. But regional and suburban rail operators warned that continued delays were possible into Wednesday morning.

events.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
events.description: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

events.title: India: What’s driving the intense pressure on students?
events.description: The reported deaths of aspiring medical students in India have exposed the immense pressures of the country's high-stakes examination system and a worsening mental-health crisis.

events.title: UN nuclear agency boss says inspectors will visit Iran's nuclear sites under Iran-US interim deal

events.title: Play puts spotlight on Kenya’s crisis of gender-based violence
events.description: Autobiographical work Free Me aims to encourage victims to speak out in country where violence against women is rising There are audible gasps in the auditorium in Nairobi as a husband launches a volley of blows and slaps on his wife and pushes her to the floor. “I wish I could spare you this,” the wife tells the audience. “My husband beat me up as if we were in a bar fight. Except, in a bar someone fights back.” The scene comes from Free Me, an autobiographical play by Gathoni Kimuyu, a Kenyan theatre and TV producer who lived through an abusive marriage. Continue reading...

events.title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
events.description: As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable

events.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears

events.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
events.description: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: France’s latest heatwave: ‘Temperatures will fall, and we won’t talk about it anymore’
events.description: The heatwave that has engulfed France is underscoring the country’s lack of preparedness for climate change. Researcher and climate scientist François Gemenne laments that this is only considered a crisis during heatwaves, saying that France urgently needs to renovate its buildings – and reconsider its aversion to air conditioning.

events.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
events.description: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) told Nathaniel Raymond , an American human rights investigator at Yale University, that “significant private pressure” from the UAE meant the UK would not publicly divulge information linking Ethiopia and the emirates to their support for the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told

events.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say

Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. events.title: Liberals dumbfounded by Angus Taylor’s multiculturalism comments: ‘Embrace the reality of modern Australia’

events.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
events.description: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak . Continue reading...

events.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
events.description: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira, whose acclaimed roles include the detective in the Netflix Japanese-British drama Giri/Haji. Pre-production begins in November, ahead of the shoot in February 2027. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive

events.title: Curacao's trailblazing doctor at the World Cup

events.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia battle past DR Congo to reach round of 32
events.description: ⁠Colombia secured ​their place in the World Cup knockouts round with a game to spare after a 1-0 victory over DR Congo pushed them back to the top of Group ​K ‌on Tuesday, with Daniel Munoz ⁠finding a winner in the 76th minute after the South Americans had been ‌frustrated for much of the evening.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six editions
events.description: Scoring a brace against Uzbekistan on Tuesday, Cristiano Ronaldo made history by becoming the first player to score in six different editions of the FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old will hope to secure Portugal a knockout stage berth, with the squad facing Colombia for their final group stage match on Saturday.

Despite dominating possession with 79 percent of the ball, England were unable to break down Ghana's resolute defensive organisation, with the Black Stars frustrating their opponents throughout.

events.title: Former Pinochet agents convicted over 1976 Washington DC carbomb murder
events.description: Attack targeted former Chile ambassador Orlando Letelier and his US colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt Fifty years after Gen Augusto Pinochet ’s secret police detonated a car bomb in the heart of Washington DC , killing Orlando Letelier, a former Chilean minister and ambassador to the US, and his American colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt, a Santiago court has convicted three former agents of Moffitt’s murder. Judge Paola Plaza, a special minister for human rights in Chile, sentenced Pedro Espinoza, José Zara, and Raúl Iturriaga to 15 years in prison for their roles in the killing of Moffitt, 25. events.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
events.description: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

events.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
events.description: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence. Continue reading...

events.title: Interest rate hikes remain on cards as Australia’s underlying inflation climbs, economists warn
events.description: Despite plunging fuel prices, trimmed mean inflation increased from an annual pace of 3.4% to 3.6% Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s inflation challenge is far from over and more Reserve Bank rate hikes remain on the cards , economists warn, even after a plunging petrol costs drove an unexpected fall in consumer price growth in the year to May. Annual inflation dropped to 4%, from 4.2% in the year to April, according to the Australian Bureau of Statistics, dragged down in large part thanks to a nearly 12% drop in fuel prices in the month of May. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Interest rate hikes remain on cards as Australia’s underlying inflation climbs, economists warn

events.title: French Resistance hero and historian Marc Bloch enters France's Pantheon
events.description: French Resistance fighter and pioneering historian Marc Bloch entered France's Pantheon on Tuesday, becoming the first historian to receive one of the nation's highest honours. The ceremony comes amid renewed political debate over his anti-nationalist legacy less than a year before France's presidential election.

events.title: Macron pays tribute to Marc Bloch and 'his teachings against the spirit of defeatism'
events.description: French Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944, entered the Pantheon on Tuesday, becoming the first historian to receive the honour bestowed on exceptional figures in politics, culture and science. French President Emmanuel Macron paid tribute the historian of Jewish heritage and "his teachings that compel us to move forward, against the spirit of defeatism".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Macron pays tribute to Marc Bloch and 'his teachings against the spirit of defeatism'

events.title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation

This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.

Trump has blamed the problems on "vandalism," claiming a 350-foot gash was deliberately cut into the pool and saying multiple arrests have been made. However, he has not publicly provided evidence for the claims, despite journalists pressing him for proof.

events.title: Australia politics live: Paul Hogan an ‘essential feature of Australian monoculture’, Hanson says
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ruston pushes for overhaul to controversial aged care tool Over to her portfolio, the shadow health and aged care minister, Anne Ruston , will introduce a private member’s bill to change the government’s controversial integrated assessment tool. The three things it seeks to do is to restore the discretion of a human assessor to make sure that the algorithm in their professional judgment doesn’t make an error. It also requires greater transparency so that every decision that’s made, the person can know how the algorithm was used, how professional judgment was applied, so they know why they received the level of care that they got. And we also want to make sure that anybody who has received an assessment since this algorithm computer-only decision-making mechanism has been in place, that they can have a reassessment because we believe that so many of the results have clearly been incorrect. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australia politics live: Paul Hogan an ‘essential feature of Australian monoculture’, Hanson says

events.description: As Europe swelters under a major heatwave, the energy-intensiveness of artificial intelligence has come into the spotlight. At a climate conference in London, UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres called on AI companies to disclose the environmental impact of their systems. Plus, Iran rejects the US's claim that any frozen Iranian assets, once unlocked, will be controlled by the US and Qatar and be used exclusively to buy American food and medicine.

events.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
events.description: Archaeologists say they have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society

events.title: South African prisons open art galleries to showcase inmates' work
events.description: In Johannesburg, a correctional facility is transforming perceptions with a gallery showcasing inmates' artwork

events.title: EU observers back Colombia vote count amid challenge to results
events.description: A European Union mission has highlighted the transparency and efficiency of Colombia's presidential election vote count

events.title: China’s newest aircraft carrier sails through the Taiwan Strait
events.description: China's newest and most powerful aircraft carrier, the Fujian, has sailed through the Taiwan Strait, according to Taiwan's Defense Ministry

events.title: Thai woman faces Myanmar court in an immigration trial tied to US diplomat’s killing
events.description: A Thai woman accused of killing her U.S. diplomat ex-husband has appeared in a Myanmar court
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai woman faces Myanmar court in an immigration trial tied to US diplomat’s killing

events.title: A legal battle over a former Zambian president's burial might be over
events.description: A South African court has ruled in favor of former Zambian President Edgar Lungu's family regarding his burial

events.title: Lithuanian government steps down after coalition reshuffle
events.description: Lithuanian Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė and her cabinet have stepped down after changes to the ruling coalition

events.title: Man arrested over collecting human body parts taken from cemeteries, police say
events.description: Police in Hungary have arrested a 30-year-old man for collecting human body parts

events.title: Estranged husband of former Scottish leader sentenced to more than 5 years in prison
events.description: The estranged husband of former Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon has been sentenced to five years and three months in prison

events.title: Afghan Taliban to hold rare, closed-door talks with EU officials on deportations
events.description: A Taliban delegation is in Brussels for talks with the European Union, focusing on deportations, according to a Taliban official

events.title: Florida man allegedly kidnapped teen girl and hid her inside a clothes dryer before she called 911
events.description: A Florida man allegedly kidnapped a teenage girl and hid her inside a clothes dryer at his Key Largo home, according to the Monroe County Sheriff's Office.

events.title: Five charged after jumping out of pickup truck to allegedly rob a man at gunpoint — for $30 vape pen: police
events.description: Five Florida men face felony robbery charges after allegedly holding up a man walking home from work and stealing a vape pen worth about $30, police say.

events.title: DHS demands New York sanctuary politicians honor detainer for man convicted of raping corpse on subway
events.description: Felix Jeronimo-Rojas, an illegal immigrant from Mexico, was sentenced to five years in prison after pleading guilty to raping the corpse of a man on the subway last year.

events.title: FBI adds 2 fugitives to 'Most Wanted Fraudsters' list amid historic $6.5B healthcare takedown: Patel
events.description: FBI adds Khalid Ahmed Satary and Emylee Thai to "Most Wanted Fraudsters" list as historic healthcare fraud crackdown charges 455 suspects for $6.5B.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's father haunted by Karmelo Anthony trial visuals released: ‘I had never seen those pictures’
events.description: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony is fighting to appeal his 35-year sentence for murdering Austin Metcalf at a Texas high school track meet in 2025.

events.title: From the Moon to Mars: Engineering the Future of Human Exploration
events.description: The journey to Mars begins with the Moon. This video explores how Lockheed Martin is helping NASA prepare for the next era of human exploration through Orion, Artemis, robotic missions, lunar resources and technologies designed to help astronauts live, work and travel safely in deep space

events.title: Turkey detains over 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in sweeping raid ahead of NATO summit
events.description: Turkey detained more than 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in a sweeping Ankara raid ahead of the July NATO summit Trump is expected to attend.

events.title: Trump gets major win against China in African rare earth minerals race
events.description: Trump administration backs American mining company Virtus Minerals in the DRC to counter China's control of critical cobalt and copper supply chains.

events.title: Trump says Colombia's 'El Tigre' will be a 'great president' as socialist opponent launches legal challenge
events.description: Trump congratulated de la Espriella on his apparent Colombia election win. With 99.9% counted, he leads 49.7% to 48.7%, but rival Cepeda has launched a challenge over voting irregularities.

events.title: Keith Kellogg tells Iranian dissidents the 'window is open' to force regime change in Tehran
events.description: Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg urged Iranian dissidents at a Paris NCRI gathering to seize a historic opening, saying the Tehran regime is weaker than ever.

events.title: Meloni's spat with Trump is calculated strategy to boost her approval ratings: expert
events.description: Analyst says Meloni's public feud with President Trump is a calculated strategy to boost her declining approval ratings ahead of Italy's 2027 general election.

events.title: Iran's unprecedented 'whole-regime' delegation at US deal talks signals one goal: expert
events.description: Iran sent a whole-regime delegation, including its central bank governor and oil officials to Switzerland talks, signaling demands for immediate cash flow.

events.title: Trump-backed 'El Tigre' looks to crush cartels, end Colombia's socialist era in pivotal election
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, known as El Tigre, rides voter frustration over crime and cartels in Colombia's high-stakes presidential runoff election.

events.title: African and Caribbean leaders call for payments, debt cancellation, formal apologies over slavery
events.description: African and Caribbean leaders adopted a 19-point reparations plan in Ghana demanding financial compensation, debt cancellation and formal apologies.

events.title: H5N1 bird flu confirmed in Australia for the first time, meaning virus has now reached every continent
events.description: Australia confirms its first case of H5N1 bird flu in a seabird near Esperance, Western Australia, meaning the virus has now reached every continent.
